INCIDENTTITLE: ASSET/PROPERTY at tuticorin site at 2023-06-23 14:30
INCIDENTDETAILS:
At around 14:22hrs Copper Duty JSO Sivakumar received an information from Security Guard Esakkiappan deployed at SLF duty post, that he noticed two anonymous persons trying to entering in between SLF and 4LTPA
Area. Immediately Duty JSO rushed to the spot along with SO Saravanakumar. Meanwhile Mr. Rajkumar-HOD and JSO Diwahar reached the spot through outside boundary wall. But miscreants left the material they are trying to steal and escaped jumping the boundary wall.  SO Saravankumar and JSO Sivakumar observed their faces from some distance while crossing the wall. The material left out by the miscreants was recovered from the site found to be- Aluminum sheets & MS rods Approx. 20kgs covered in the bag. The same was deposited at material gate security office. 

On 24th June at 10.00 hrs Mr Rajkumar-HOD notices few people crossing copper plant material gate with same identity which was noticed in the previous day theft attempt. Immediately he stopped those personnel with support of material gate police. On detailed enquired of those personnel, it was revealed that those are the same personnel involved in the theft attempt in the previous day at 4LTPA. Further, SO Saravankumar and JSO Sivakumar also confirmed the same peoples were entered into 4LTPA area in the previous day. On confirmation those personnel Marriappan age -30,  Sudali, Age-33 both are from Tsunami colonoy, Silvampatti- Therespuram, were handed over to SIPCOT Police Station for further action at their end.

INCIDENTTITLE: IR at RDC site at 15 Feb 2024, 1:12 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS: On 15.01.2024 at around 1250 hrs. around 15 local leaders (Mahendra Maan, Badrilal Menaria, Hari Singh etc.) and villagers reached at SLF gate and blocked the gate demanding employment of locals in DSC plant resulting into stoppage of all movement through SLF gate. On 16.01.2024 again at around 0411 hrs. the same people along with some other leaders (Kotdi Sarpanch Abhishek Choudhary) and villagers (around 55-55 persons) gathered in front of DSC main gate and blocked all the gates of DSC resulting into stoppage of all man/material movement, stoppage of plant. The police team has reached the site to manage law and order situations. After repeated round of meeting with senior management and local leaders the strike has been called off at around 1525 hrs. After that shift movement and material movement was started but workers have been stopped by union leaders stating that to 23 VRS worker who are working at CPP to be relocated to lead plant. After discussion with the management and union representatives the strike was called off at around 1720 hrs. All movement are normal afterwards
LNAME: SLF & Jarofix yard and SS workshop near SLF

Upon noticing this activity, the guard attempted to intervene, prompting the miscreants to flee from the Ash Dyke where the materials were stored. Subsequently, the guard alerted the shift in-charge at Plant-1 about the situation. Following the alert, the security team swiftly proceeded to the location and discovered that the miscreants had absconded, leaving behind a vehicle registered as CG12H3377. The security team secured and relocated the abandoned vehicle from the Ash Dyke to the parking area of Plant-1 for further investigation.

INCIDENTTITLE: IR (Protest)at RDM site at 30 Jul 2023, 3:00 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
Protest by local villagers and MLA Rajsamand - On date 30/07/2023, From 1500hrs to 1930hrs Rajsamand MLA Mrs. Deepti Maheshwari with approx 150 local villagers and Kotari Sarpanch gathered at RD gate due to allegations of tailing and zerofix spread over of near by fields of SKM,RDM & DSC.There after discussion happened with IBU CEO and BU heads in which it was decided to do the survey of affected area within 5 days. Secondly the RO installed by HZL at different locations should provide water without any charge upto 20ltr. Per family.
Thirdly the tailing spread over in nearby fields should be taken out by HZL within 2 months.
Protest ended at 1930hrs.
Police team was roped in during the protest.
The protest ended peacefully.

INCIDENTTITLE: ASSET/PROPERTY at BALCO site at 2023-06-07 11:50
INCIDENTDETAILS: On June 07, 2023, at around 1150 hrs. While carrying out CCTV surveillance of perimeter cameras, Surveillance Operator Mr. Somnath Dehri, observed miscreant's movement behind BLC building. CSOC immediately informed Plant 03 Security-Incharge Mr. Ajay Kumar Singh, along with the Quick Response Team . The security teams quickly rushed towards the location and cordoned off the area mean while Township QRT team with local police was sent to the location from outside to cover the fox hole. Some miscreants managed to flee away taking advantage of uneven terrain and dense vegetation however security team was able to apprehend 01 miscreant red-handed and recovered approx. 30kg iron scrap . Deori, a security personnel, was informed and dispatched to WT-8 for an ambush. INCIDENTTITLE: IR at RDC site at 2023-06-14 10:40
INCIDENTDETAILS: With prior intelligence information, SK Mines security team blocked the SK Ghati Gate by barricades and security vehicles to prevent unauthorized entry in Mines premises. Around 10:45hrs, Kishan Gadari S/o Gopi Lal Gadari and Ratan Gadari S/o Gopi Lal Gadari along with family members including ladies around 10 residents of Sunariya Kheda village, came to SK Ghati regarding land contamination related issues at Tailing Dam site, with two tailing dam soil loaded tractors and tried to enter SK Mines from Ghati Gate but Security team stopped them at Ghati gate by blocking gate. INCIDENTTITLE: ASSET/PROPERTY at BALCO site at 2023-09-17 21:30
INCIDENTDETAILS: On September 17, 2023, at approximately 2130 hrs., Mr. Jitendra Gupta, the Plant-1 shift incharge, along with the deployed SG (Mr. Deepak Minz, Mr. Milkius Tirkey, and Mr. Tek Bahadur Rana) observed the suspicious activity of 3-4 individuals Behind BLC ,near the AHP Soil dump area Plant-3. Without any delay, they promptly relayed this information to the CSOC (Centralized Security Operations Centre). In response to the report, the CSOC immediately dispatched a QRT (Quick Response Team) to the specified location. The security team chased the miscreants, but as they observed the security team approaching, the miscreants resorted to pelting stones at our security personnel. During this confrontation, one of our security guards, Mr. Tek Bahadur Rana, sustained a minor facial injury due to the stone pelting. He was immediately sent to Balco Hospital for further treatment. However, the miscreants managed to escape from the location, taking advantage of low illumination and wild vegetation. They left behind approximately 50-60 kg of stolen iron scrap. Fortunately, our security team successfully recovered the material from the scene and Police compliant logged against the miscreants.

INCIDENTTITLE: Access Control at BALCO site at 15 May 2023, 8:15 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS: On May 15th, 2023, the Intelligence team received information regarding an incident of proxy punching involving Mr. Yogendra Pratap Singh, a Site Engineer at M/S MBPL with UID No. 1596231. It was alleged that Yogendra Pratap Singh was gaining unauthorized access to the plant by using someone else's access card. Upon further investigation, it was found that Mr. Yogendra Pratap Singh had lost his own access card on May 2nd, 2023. As he was the site in charge, he knew that Mr. Shiv Pratap Singh, with UID No. 1596257, was on leave from April 29th, 2023, due to medical reasons. In an attempt to find his own access card, Yogendra Pratap Singh had grabbed Mr. Shiv Pratap Singh's access card without informing anyone. Although he couldn't find his card. However, Yogendra Pratap Singh continued to use Mr. Shiv Pratap Singh's access card for day-to-day work without informing anyone. He gained access to the plant using Mr. Shiv Pratap Singh's access card for work on May 4th, 5th, 8th, 9th, 10th, and 15th, 2023, for a total of four days.

INCIDENTTITLE: ASSET/PROPERTY at BALCO site at 2023-06-21 18:16
INCIDENTDETAILS: On 21st June 2023, at approximately 1819 hours, while carrying out routine surveillance of the perimeter cameras, surveillance operator Mr. Somnath Dehri observed miscreants movement near Watch Tower 07, Plant-1. Two miscreants gained unauthorized access to the facility by scaling the boundary wall with the help of a ladder. The miscreants removed the cover of the drainage pipeline and helped the 3rd miscreant to gain access inside the facility and targeted the scrap of old Alumina plant. CSOC immediately notified the Quick Response Team (QRT), Plant-1 shift Incharge, Mr. Manish Chaudhary & OSD, Mr. Raghav regarding the same. Security team immediately responded by rushing quickly to the reported location. However, upon the arrival of the security team, the miscreants escaped from the area through the drainage pipeline leaving behind the stolen material. Security team searched the nearby area and recovered iron scrap approx. 70 kg. from the drainage pipeline.

INCIDENTTITLE: ASSET/PROPERTY at BALCO site at 05 Sep 2023, 2:00 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS: On September 5th, 2023, we were informed of theft incidents involving the pilferage of PPEs from the Foundry's Store, located within the foundry area. The report was provided by the Store In charge, Mr. Gaya Prasad. Upon receiving this information, our security team-initiated a thorough investigation by analyzing the CCTV footage. The review revealed that between 1300 to 1400 hours, during Mr. Gaya Prasad's lunch break, two individuals consistently accessed the store. They would open the store, remove PPEs, and then securely lock it afterward. Subsequent analysis identified the culprits as Mr. Kishor (UID: 20985), an apprentice working under Mr. Gaya Prasad, and Mr. Amrendra Singh (UID: 1546278 M/s Surendra Engineering). Mr. Kishor's familiarity with the store's key placement allowed him to access it during Mr. Gaya Prasad's absence. He would then contact Mr. Amrendra Singh, who would collect the stolen PPEs. After retrieving the PPEs, Mr. Amrendra Singh would proceed directly to the melting area, while Mr. Kishor returned to the In-charge office to reposition the keys at their designated location. This pattern of theft began on July 23rd and continued July 25th, 27th, 28th, and 31st. The thefts also extended into August, occurring on the 7th, 8th, 9th, and 12th of the month. Additionally, it has come to our attention that the stolen PPEs were subsequently sold by the perpetrators to another worker, Mr. Surendra Kumar Kaushik (UID: 1545518 M/s Parekh Engineering). Mr. Kaushik established a fixed price for various PPE items, offering 200 rupees each for Face Shields and Gloves and 500 rupees for each Balaclava.

INCIDENTDETAILS: On May 19th, 2023, while monitoring CCTV footage & analysing the physical access data, an incident of Unauthorized entry and Proxy punching was detected. The investigation revealed that Mr. Vikash Pandey (UID No. 1581026) from M/s. Western Carriers Pvt. Ltd. had misused his gate pass as well as the gate pass of Mr. Ajeet Pandey (UID 104980) to allow the entry of Mr. Brijesh (an outsider), Mr. Kanaiya Sahu (UID 1543937), and Surjit (UID 1546921), who are workers of M/s. Western Carriers Pvt. Ltd. Moreover, Mr. Kanaiya Sahu and Mr. Surjit normally worked in the Finish Good operation for rake loading. However, on 18th May 2023, Mr. Vikas Pandey instructed them to complete their shifts using their own access cards and then asked them to work in the growth project area at the Project site in C-shift. He provided Mr. Brijesh and Mr. Kanaiya Sahu access using Mr. Ajeet Singh's access card, and Mr. Surjit Das was given access through Mr. Vikas Pandey's access card. The Security Team conducted a thorough investigation and called Mr. Vikash Pandey for further investigation.

INCIDENTTITLE: ASSET/PROPERTY at BALCO site at 2023-05-19 04:20
INCIDENTDETAILS: On May 19th, 2023, at approximately 0420 hrs, Mr. Hadees (Electrical operations in charge at the DM Plant), noticed a flashover in the perimeter cable from the 1200 MW control room. While checking with the ground team, he found that the parameter cable had been cut at the DM Plant. Mr. Hadees immediately reported the incident to the CSOC (Central Security Operations Centre). In response, the CSOC sent the Quick Response Team (QRT) to the location. However, the miscreants managed to escape due to dense vegetation and inadequate lighting. Subsequently, the intelligence team began analysing the CCTV footage. During the analysis, they discovered an image of the suspects, which helped them identify the point where the individuals entered and exited the premises. The intelligence team shared the image of the suspects within their network to identify them, and within a short period of time, they successfully identified the two individuals as Shubham Singh and Rakesh Sahani. Further investigation revealed that both individuals were employed by a subcontractor of M/S NGSL, that had previously worked at the DM Plant.

INCIDENTTITLE: ASSET/PROPERTY at BALCO site at 2023-06-13 16:50
INCIDENTDETAILS: On June 13, 2023, at approximately 16:50 hours, an intelligence report was received indicating that a group of local residents had gained unauthorized access to the facility by entering through a foxhole in the boundary wall Behind BLC area. CSOC promptly notified the Quick Response Team (QRT) regarding the same & the security team swiftly responded by rushing to the reported location. However, upon the arrival of the security team, the miscreants quickly fled from the area. Subsequently, an ambush was planned by the Intelligence team in collaboration with the QRT, and the Traffic team was also called in for reinforcement. The security team concealed themselves in the surrounding bushes and, after waiting for approximately one hour, detected signs of intrusion. The team swiftly advanced towards the miscreants who had specifically targeted the scrap in the dumping area. Although some miscreants managed to escape by taking advantage of the uneven terrain and dense vegetation, the security team successfully apprehended two individuals red-handed and recovered approx. 50 kg iron scrap. The miscreants were identified as Mr. Roshan Kumar Mahant S/o Mr. Ram Kumar Mahant & Mr. Sonu Kumar Ourav S/O Mr. Pardeshi Ram, residents of Belgari Basti Parsabhata. The captured miscreants were handed over to the Balco Police station, and a police complaint was lodged against them by the security team for their attempted infiltration.

INCIDENTTITLE: ASSET/PROPERTY at RAM Agucha site at 30 Nov 2023, 7:00 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
An incident involving the theft of diesel from a crane, Reg. No.RJ14EA5119, owned by M/S Monomark. On 30/11/2023 at about the 1900hrs , we received reliable intelligence regarding the occurrence of diesel theft from a specific crane located near the patrol pump at Rupaheli Circle Corner. In response, our Quick Response Team (QRT) promptly mobilized, accompanied by Mr. Mittusingh, the Administrative Officer of the security agency M/S G4S .
Upon reaching the reported location, our team noticed the crane heading towards the mine and immediately pursued it. They successfully tracked the crane to the mine circle, where they apprehended the driver. Subsequently, an investigation led by Deputy CSO Ajay Sharma was initiated.
The investigation revealed that the driver, identified as Harman Singh From Gurdaspur (Punjab) with license number PB5820200000161, accepted responsibility for pilfering approximately 60 liters of diesel. He confessed to selling the stolen diesel to a local shop owner who specializes in vehicle repairs. Harman Singh, a resident of Gurdaspur, Punjab, has been entering the site using a service engineer gate pass for the past 15 days. It is worth noting that the crane is operated by M/S Shree Ganesh Crane Services, a subcontractor of M/S Monomark.
The incident took place on 30/11/2023 when the crane was dispatched to the ZLD plant located outside the mine at approximately 1600 hrs on proper NRGP. However, the driver deviated from the assigned task and diverted to Rupaheli Circle at around 1900 hrs with the intention of theft. As a result, the driver has been handed over to the Gulabpura Police Station for further investigation and legal proceedings.

INCIDENTTITLE: Access Control at RDC site at 2023-09-09 22:01
INCIDENTDETAILS:
Bhupendra Paliwal of TCL company came around 15:36 hrs. After punch-in, He waited for a minute and went out after punch-out (time – 15:37 hrs.). Then again, he came back around 22:01 hrs. and asked security supervisor Ramesh Chandra of M/S G4S (deployed at turnstile gate) to allow him manually. The supervisor asked him that, from where he is coming and how did he go outside? On asking him, he told the supervisor that, he went out after punch-out to have food. After that supervisor instructed security guard Mukesh Kumar of M/S G4S to open the side gate and allow him manually through gate without punch-in. After that, Bhupendra Paliwal punched out and went outside. The supervisor didn’t ask or inform ASO/HR/ security in-charge and made the decision by himself. On supervisor’s instruction security guard opened the gate and Bhupendra Paliwal entered inside without punch-in.  
Investigation report sent to the concerned person for further action.

INCIDENTTITLE: ASSET/PROPERTY at BALCO site at 2023-07-29 00:11
INCIDENTDETAILS: On 29th July, 2023 at approximately 0011 hrs., during CCTV surveillance ,Surveillance Operator Mr. Somnath Deheri Observed suspicions movement of 4 miscreants in APP In area, Plant-1. Immediately CSOC notified the Quick Response Team (QRT), Plant-1 shift Incharge Mr. Ranjeet Jha and Plant-2 shift incharge Mr. Sonu Painkra. Additionally, the Rake loading guard, Railway Gate guard, and CH-2 guard were also alerted. Security team responded to the site of incident and with support from CSOC, tracked the movement of miscreants. However, upon the arrival of the security team, miscreants escaped the area through the fox hole by empty handed.

INCIDENTTITLE: ASSET/PROPERTY at BALCO site at 2023-08-01 06:02
INCIDENTDETAILS: On 01st August, 2023 at around 0602 hrs., during CCTV surveillance, Mr. Harigendra Singh Parihar (Surveillance Operator ) observed suspicious movement in Washer Settler Workshop area, Plant-1 (03 miscreants). The Central Security Operations Centre (CSOC) immediately informed Mr. Sonu Painkra, shift In Charge of Plant-1. Simultaneously, the Quick Response Team (QRT) was redirected to the location from outside. While the CSOC kept tracking the movements of the miscreants, the security team promptly responded to the incident site. However, by the time security team arrived, miscreants had managed to flee from the area. Nonetheless, security team successfully recovered the stolen items, which included a ladder and approximately 250 kg of Iron scrap.

INCIDENTTITLE: ASSET/PROPERTY at BALCO site at 2023-08-04 22:00
INCIDENTDETAILS: On 04.08.2023 at around 2200 hrs., while frisking at Parsabhata Out Gate during "B" Shift exit Plant 1 Shift I/C Mr. Raghav Tomar caught Mr. Ramlal Yadav(UID - 7168), a worker of M/s Shri Jogendra Engineering Enterprises in act of Pilfering of Packing material of Wire-rod from Cast House 3 He was frisked by the Plant 1 Shift I/C & on further investigation, it was discovered that he was working at Cast House 3 and this packing material are commonly used to pack Wire-rod in that location thereby proving that the material was being removed with the intention of stealing. Mr. Ramlal Yadav was caught red-handed at Parsabhata Gate. The statement was recorded and gatepass submitted to HR Team for further necessary action .

A group consisting of 4-5 individuals was observed engaging in the theft of iron rods. The security team promptly pursued the miscreants. Upon the security team's approach, the intruders swiftly fled the scene, abandoning 11 iron rods, each measuring approximately 35-40 feet in length. Immediate communication was established with the Quick Response Team (QRT) and the Night Duty Officer (NDO), Mr. Rajesh Gauda, to manage the situation. On December 26, 2023, the pilfered materials were successfully transported to the L&T site using a crane, with assistance from L&T laborers. The entire operation was conducted under the vigilant supervision of the Plant 1 Shift Incharge.

INCIDENTTITLE: Workers strike due to salary delay at SILVASSA site at 05 Mar 2024, 6:00 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
On 05th March 2023, at about 0600hrs around 35 monomark employees of A shift gathered near main gate and refused to go on work, due to non-payment of salary wages. Immediately B Madhan Kumar along with QRT and security team deployed at main gate. Security officer passed information to the Head Security, and Security SIC informed to the CCPC SIC, Refinery SIC and CCR SIC. OHC informed by Control room operator. Head security informed to the BU COO, IR and HR head. At 0630hrs, around 10 C-shift employees of Monomark also joined the gathering at main gate. At 0709hrs Monomark Site Head Mr Prashant Naik reported and convinced workers to go to work stating that their salary will happen EOD. Further, at 0712hrs Monomark employees reported to duty peacefully. At 0915hrs, around 15 G-shift Monomark employees gathered outside main gate, Security SIC informed to the Head Security, Head HR and Head IR. At 0930hrs Monomark HR Vaishali Pandya, and Prashant Naik convinced them and further they reported to their duty.

On 06th March 2023, 0610hrs 50-60 Monomark Employees gathered near main gate since their wages did not credit as promised by management. Security team deployed at the gate immediately. At 0620hrs necessary communication done to the Head HR, Head PR, COO and Monomark Management. At 0645hrs Peregrine area manager also reached at the site. At 0654hrs Mr Prashant Naik reached and tried to convince workers, but workers refused to go back on work. At 0808hrs Head PR Mr Joshi and Head HR Mr Saket reached on the site and stated that wages would be credited by 7th March 2023. Further, Monomark personal resumed duty peacefully at 0830hrs.

INCIDENTTITLE: Attempt of Gate block
INCIDENTDETAILS:
At around 1350 hrs 13 people of Bhandari Band village had come to Karma gate and told the gate commander that they wanted to meet Mr. Sanjay Sinha regarding job issue. 
Post receiving the information the Security Shift IC talked to Mr Sanjay Sinha regarding their issue but he denied to meet him. Around 1515 hrs they got aggressive and tried to block the gate but the alert security team stopped the attempt. Post meeting with Mr Sandeep of PR department all went back at 1605 hrs. A meeting has been scheduled after Holi for sorting out the issue.

INCIDENTTITLE: ASSET/PROPERTY at BALCO site at 07 Jan 2024, 9:17 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS: At around 0917 hrs., on January 7, 2024, an intrusion alarm triggered by perimeter video analytics alerted the CSOC team. Swiftly verifying the alarm, CSOC detected unauthorized movement involving 05-06 miscreants near Watch Tower 07 of Plant 1. During the intrusion attempt, 01 of the miscreants was attempting to enter the premises through the perimeter wall. Simultaneously, the Quick Response Team (QRT) was patrolling the area. Upon noticing security team, the miscreants hastily fled the location. CSOC promptly alerted the Plant 1 team who were dispatched from outside to apprehend the miscreants but unfortunately the miscreants managed to escape before interception.

INCIDENTTITLE: ASSET/PROPERTY at BALCO site at 10 Jan 2024, 1:00 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS: On January 10, 2024, around 0100 hours, Mr. Tek Bahadur Rai, the security guard, identified 6-7 intruders in the mill area during his patrol. He promptly reported this to CSOC, who informed the Plant 1 In Charge. The Quick Response Team (QRT) was swiftly redirected to the location, arriving at approximately 0124 hours. They cordoned off the area and initiated a thorough search operation. Taking advantage of darkness and dense vegetation, the miscreants fled upon noticing the security team, leaving behind stolen materials. During the search operation, the security team successfully recovered approximately 11 bundles of copper cables, each weighing around 30 kg.

INCIDENTTITLE: Foiled Theft Attempt : Brass Metals - Zinc Cell House - DSC
INCIDENTDETAILS:
On 29/03/2024 at around 1740 hrs. a worker came to turnstile out gate for exit. During frisking by security through HHMD some metals were detected. Thorough checking was conducted and around 10 Kgs of brass metals (1 rod, 1 bearing and 2 bushes) has been recovered. The approx. cost of the material is Rs. 3000/-.

The worker has been interrogated and he has admitted that he has stolen the materials from Zinc Cell House, Zinc Plant. During interrogation it is suspected that the individual was under an intoxicated condition. Alcohol check has been conducted and found negative. Upon further suspicion, he has been sent to hospital for drugs test where the result was found positive.  Statement of the worker has been recorded and mail communicated to HR and concerned dept. for blacklist the worker and further necessary action.

INCIDENTTITLE: Traffic at VAL J site at 14 Nov 2023, 1:10 AM

The incident occurred as the coal driver slept while driving. There was no injury reported. Both the vehicles' wind shields were broken. The coal vehicle's left side window and bonnet was damaged. The ash vehicle's front bonnet and side window was damaged. Alcohol was tested and the result was negative. Challan was issued to the coal driver as per the SOP. Statements were obtained from the drivers. Both the drivers were sent to the DMC for counselling and their vehicles were kept on hold till further action. Both the drivers' papers were held with the Duty Officer for further investigation.

INCIDENTTITLE: Any Other at IRON ORE ORISSA site at 14 Dec 2023, 12:00 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
Theft Incident at IOO: Pilferage of 5/18mm CLO at Nadidihi Iron & Mn Ore mine, IOO dated 14.12.2023.

Incident Reporting: 
The PC operator known as Amulya Munda informed the Logistics shift Incharge, Mr.Lal Babu Rai  between 00. 15 hrs to 00.30 hrs(15.12.2023) that one Truck, No. NL01AE6260 (22-wheeler) came to the weighbridge heavily overloaded carrying CLO 5-18mm. This truck came to the weighbridge no 4 platform but went without doing any weight adjustment. He noticed that the external display of the weighbridge was OFF.  He expected that this truck would come back to the weighbridge for weighment. When this truck did not come back for weighment but went outside the gate, he got suspicious and hence informed the Logistic Supervisor incharge of the Shift Mr. Lal Babu Rai who was present at the Security cum Document printing Cabin where documents are printed.    

Lal Babu Rai promptly took the security QRT team assistance and started chasing.  Meanwhile they contacted the transporter and asked to Hold the truck and informed the Transporter not to make the LR.  The Transporter confirmed that they have informed the driver not to proceed. The team found the truck parked at Bhadrasahi (about 20 km) from the mine. The Driver did not respond to the calls from the Transporters after this incident. 

The team decided to get back the truck to the mine & the Truck was brought back and parked at the mines by arranging other driver(by transporter) at about 3.00 am on 15th Dec. The Truck weighed 18 tons more than the Gross weight on the Documents. 

The truck was dispatched with TP No: L22301186/11.  Gross weight: 56.940, Net weight 40.140.   TP timing: 11:26pm   Name of the Consigner: Red Mountain Minerals (trader), End User: Anindita Steels Limited Senegarh, Jharkand. 

As the truck was found to be carrying ore more than that mentioned in the Transit permit, it was decided by IOO Exco that the case needs to be investigated. 


Investigating team:   Camilo Fernandes with Logistics Team, Uday Verma -Security, Vivek Sambrekar (Operations), Jaidev Mandal – IT (for video footage from Weighbridge Camera).

Investigation Proceedings:  
1)	All 4 weighbridges at the mine are manned by NSIPL staff for Transit pass generation. There is no IOO persons at the weighbridges all the time and only the IOO supervisor on duty makes rounds on need basis or casual rounds.  The Third-party persons deployed from our end(AD-ashtra) are engaged for document printing(Transit Pass, invoice, e-way bill) at Exit point/Security gate.  
2)	The weighbridge Operator (Dharmendra Kanungo, employee of NSIPL) on duty at the time of Incidence went absconding as soon as the case came in to light and before the truck was bought back.   
3)	The PC 71 operator at W/B known as Amulya Munda (deployed by NSIPL) was called to the mines in the morning(15.12.23) and he narrated the same incident as described above.  He further said that one more Truck 4073 approached him to give him more tonnage or said something which he could not understand but he adjusted the load as per process and the truck went of the weighbridge.  He said he would sign this statement once ready and wanted to go back immediately for some personal exigencies. 
4)	The Security gate CCTV Camera footage was checked and it was seen that this truck-6260 entered the Mine at around 20.00 hrs (Security ingate physical record is 19.53 hrs of 14th Dec). 
5)	There is no Security CCTV footage after Entry. The next footage of this truck on Security CCTV shows the Exit time around 23.32 hrs 
6)	In line with the narration of incidence by PC operator at Weigh bridge, the loading Operator Dushmanto Mahanto at stack and the Site Supervisor Mr.Dulal Kumar Pani were asked about the incident. As per both of them there was nothing unusual about the truck that loaded 5-18 CLO but Wheel loader operator Mahanto faintly remembers that one truck was asking for extra load and he just loaded half a bucket and asked the driver to go as he was sure that the truck was loaded enough (by his experience). He refused to accept that he can overload any truck to the extent of 18 tons.  
7)	The site Supervisor said that he does not keep a record of trucks loaded at a site but just keeps a count of trucks and that 12 trucks were loaded from 2 pm till 12 pm.
8)	Other concerned supervisors in the rank at NSIPL also said that there was no abnormal or unusual matter on that day regarding truck loading at CLO stack as only 11 to 12 trucks were loaded. The Mine Incharge of NSIPL Mr.Durga Prasad reiterate internal opinion that seeing the truck load which is evenly spread, the same is difficult to achieve with Wheel loader and it looks to be the work of excavator. He confirms that there was no excavator deployed for loading the truck in the second shift for loading 5-18 CLO. Only one truck was loaded with excavator that too in shift 3. 
9)	Mr. Jaidev Mandal was then asked to retrieve the CCTV camera footage from the weighbridge 4. It may be noted that these WB camera footages get recorded locally at weigh bridge and not centrally.
10)	Observation from WB CCTV Camera: 
a)	WB CCTV camera timing has a lag of 12 to 13 minutes from the i3MS time.
b)	The Particular truck 6260 came to the WB platform at around 22.15.  The indicator display goes off.  The WB operator seems to be communicating with the PC operator and the PC operator immediately starts to load the truck abnormally and adds about 25 to 30 buckets to load the truck fully till the container top evenly spread. The trucks moves out of the weighbridge at around 22.40 hrs (WB camera time). However as per i3MS records there is no transaction of this truck during this time interval (after adjustment of time difference). So it is inferred that the transaction was not made at that point of time. 
c)	Two more trucks weighed including truck no. 4073. It is seen that some discussion taking place with the diver of MH12UM-4073, the Weighbridge Operator and the PC Operator. 
d)	It is later seen that around 23.11 hrs one truck RJ47GA4438 (having the 55 GVW capacity vs  NL01AE6260 ie 57 tons ) destined to Bokaro enters the weighbridge Platform, the PC operator starts to adjust the weight and the truck leaves the WB platform at 23.24 hrs .   (during this time duration considering the time lag, it is seen that two transactions (ie TP challan of two trucks, one of NL01AE6260 and other of RJ47GA4438) are created. Hence it is inferred that the Weighbrdge Operator has made two transactions keeping RJ47GA4438 on W/B. First transaction is made of NL01AE6260 and then within a minute of RJ47GA4438. 
e)	On 19th Dec, the PC operator has given a statement that is recorded in camera - He says that after the display went off, the Weighbridge operator asked him to load till he instructs to stop.  He has done accordingly but as he sensed something wrong, he reported the incident to our people at security gate. He delayed to inform as there were continuous trucks reporting at the weighbridge for top-up.
f)	MDO ie NSIPL site incharge Mr.Prashant Sinha has seen the video footage on 18th Dec 23 and agreed the involvement of his staff i.e the WB operator and the PC operator. Prior to this, on 16th evening the incident was communicated to MDO owner Mr. Vinay Passary for looking into the matter.
g)	On 19th Dec 23, the Trader Red Mountain Minerals/Transporter engaged by Trader M/s Hindustan Transport and our MDO are asked to give a written statement on this incident. Response from the trader received at around18hrs on 19.12.2023.

Findings and Root Cause  

1)	Three people seem to be involved in this pilferage case. The WB-4 Operator Dharmendra Kanugo, The Truck driver, and the PC operator.
2)	The involvement of any other person not established in the investigation till now. MDO incharge, Mr. Sinha mentioned that they will investigate the matter further by taking help of Police without lodging FIR.
3)	There are loopholes in the system as the truck ID is fed manually

INCIDENTTITLE: Material at VGCB site at 26 Nov 2023, 4:00 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
On 26/11/2023 at around 16:00 hrs, 3 people were trying to stoal scrap material from CCR GATE NO-5 Outside (Near CISF Gate). The guard on patrolling noticed the same and immediatedly informed to the Supervisor who altered the QRT and able to catch one accused.
Root Cause: "a) No illumination present in the area at night.
b) The complete area is not under any Camera. Coverage. There is no Camera to Monitor CISF Gate area.
c) Scrap material is kept in open and is not under any lock and key.
d) Entry fence to the area is broken and needs to be repaired from VPT Side.
e) No Security Guard deployed particularly for that area."
"Corrective Action -
a) Security Infrastructure(Barbed wire) strengthening work has started.Barricading is being strengthened using own resources.
STATUS-U/P
b) Procurement of Additional camera has been planned as part of Capex 25 .
STATUS-U/P
c) 02 * 150 W lights are being planned to be placed in the area.
STATUS-U/P
Preventive Action -
d) Patrolling frequency as per patrolling plan has been increased in the area.
STATUS-Implemented
e) Shifting of Scrap material to a safer place (Under Lock and Key) is being planned .
STATUS-U/P"

INCIDENTTITLE: Others at VGCB site at 14 Oct 2023, 10:45 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
"At 2245h ,02 * people were seen stealing some material from TT4 and throwing the material downwards .The individuals climbed on top of TT4 and started throwing material  downwards one after the other. On noticing the same CCTV operator immediately informed the security supervisor who alerted the QRT .The QRT reached the spot at 2310h .By that time the individuals had fled with the material. The u/m material was stolen (As per mechanical team )-
1.	50mm Angles of various size : approx.62m
    2.	Grating of size: 1m x 0.8 m – 2 nos.
    3.	Hand rail pipes of 32mm size: 12m"
Root Cause: a) Limited illumination present in the area at night.
b) The complete area is not under any Camera Coverage. There is no Camera to Monitor TT4 area.
c) Scrap material is kept open in TT4  and is not under any lock and key.
d) No Security Guard deployed particularly for that area.
Corrective Action: 
1. 02 * Cameras to be procured to bring the complete area under surveillance .
STATUS-Under Progress
2.  Illumination to be enhanced in the area (02*150W lights).
STATUS-Under Progress
3.Frequency of patrolling to be increased in the area.
STATUS-Implemented
Preventive Action:
4. Mechanical team instructed to keep the material under lock and key.
STATUS-Under Process
5. CCTV Operator to be more vigilant and proactive in responding.
6.Response time of QRT should be reduced.

INCIDENTTITLE: Theft attempt of electrical equipment at SILVASSA site at 20 Jun 2023, 6:08 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS: On 20th June 2023, at 18.08hrs, its was understood that electrical work was assigned to Mr. Raunak and Mr.  Divyesh- electrical workmen from Monomark- at car parking yard. As per Mr. Raunak, electrical material (02 LED lights 100W, 03 small tubelights-5W and one 1.5mm copper cable of 50m approx.) was taken from the mini store at DG area and taken out of the plant for the said work, after declaring at the main gate for light fixing in dismantled parking yard. While coming in the plant SG Safik asked about the same, for which Mr. Raunak could not reply satisfactorily. SG Safik informed shift IC Nandkishore Patel about the same. Sensing some foul play, Nandkishore  immediately set out to track the whereabouts of the material. After investigation and questioning the custodians of the material, Nandkishore suspected the material to be in locker room. Both the person’s lockers were checked, and the material was retrieved from Mr. Raunak’s locker. On interrogation, Raunak accepted his misdoing. Same he admitted in his statement. The material was brought to security cabin and follow up investigation was done. Both the persons submitted their statements to SIC.

INCIDENTTITLE: Theft attempt of blister lug piece at SILVASSA site at 28 Sep 2023, 7:46 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS: On 28th June 2023, SIC Nandkishore Patel received an intel regarding suspicious activity about Mr. Ramkawal yadav which may happen near CCPC ground floor area. Knowing this, he deployed an ambush SG Shafik Khan at Finance office for vigilance. At 19:46hrs, it was observed that Mr. Ramkawal Yadav, forklift operator, parked the fork lift near lug cutting area at CCPC and sneaked into the lug cutting area after turning off his forklift. He placed a piece of cloth on a copper lug piece and picked the piece up. Further, he hidden the lug piece at the fork of his forklift, he took the forklift and parked it near the CCPC washroom area. He stopped his forklift, took the hidden material out from the fork, hid the piece inside his safety helmet, and he was about to sneak into the washroom. Right in front of the washroom, at 19:52hrs, he was caught by Mr. Nandkishore Patel and SG Shafik Khan, and the material was recovered from his possession. He was interrogated in the Security cabin head Security Ananda and SIC Nandkishore, to which he confessed and provided his statement to the SIC where he accepted his mistake.

INCIDENTTITLE: Scuffle between 3 persons in ACP washroom at SILVASSA site at 11 Sep 2023, 11:15 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS: On 11/09/2023 at 11:15 am Mr. Jay Prakash from fabrication mechanical went to the ACP rest room to refresh himself, where PMB helper Mr. Ajit Wagh was washing his face in the wash basin. During that time water splashed on Jay Prakash who was standing near the wash basin. A heated argument ensued between the two and Ajit Wagh groped Jayprakash’s neck tightly, with his hand. At that moment, Mr.Jaglal, fabrication mechanical, co-worker of Jayprakash, walked into the washroom and found them scuffling. Jaglal interfered into the fight, and an argument started between the Ajit Wagh and Jaglal. Then Ajit Wagh caught hold of Jaglal’s neck tightly. Jaglal, feeling suffocated, bit Ajit on his right arm very hard. Following the fight, Ajit Wagh told Jaglal and Jayprakash that this should be reported to the supervisor, but both Jaglal and Jayprakash refused to do so. Ajit Wagh complained to the Monomark supervisor about the events, and the communication reached the Security Shift Officer via the fabrication security guard. The investigation was concluded, with all three persons accepting their misdoings.

INCIDENTTITLE: Scuffle in refinery plate area at SILVASSA site at 15 Sep 2023, 7:00 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS: At 19.05 hours the security team was notified regarding a scuffle that ensued between two Monomark employees working in the Refinery, Mr. Raju Devidas Jadhao and Mr. Ajay Prasad. Both were involved in a verbal duel, followed by physically holding each other’s necks, which was resolved by a VL employee. After investigation at gate, it was found that Raju had asked Ajay to wear apron while shifting plates, due to which Ajay used curse words at him, which led to a verbal fight, and eventually held each other’s neck. We were also notified that Mr. Raju was consuming tobacco during the incident, which was confirmed during investigation, when we found the packet of tobacco in his possession. Statements from both the people were taken, and both have admitted to their mistakes.

INCIDENTTITLE: Foil Theft attempt of Copper Scrap at SILVASSA site at 18 Oct 2023, 9:45 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
On 17th October 2023, vehicle no TN52J3664 of Jain recycling Pvt, carrying copper scrap reported at Chinchpada parking area. Along with Vehicle 02 drivers and 01 security guards reported at site. On date 18.10.23 at about 9.45 PM traffic marshal deployed in parking yard reported fight between two drivers namely Mr. Jayaprakash M and Mr. Vijayakumar G. Immediately he informed the same to Shift IC Security and Logistics; both reported on the spot. Further it was understood that both the drivers and security guard (Jain recycling) was in drunken condition when they were checked for intoxication. As nobody was available to take vehicle inside, same was the reason for their fight, in this, driver Vijayakumar G threatened Driver Jayaprakash M with knife, which was confiscated by security on the spot. Further, it was revealed by one of the drivers Mr. Jayaprakash M that other driver Mr. Vijayakumar G had hidden some copper material inside the vehicle. Immediately Security team searched the vehicle, and it was observed that some copper scrap material was placed on the roof of the drivers’ s cabin hidden below the ropes and tarpaulin, same was weighing around 18 Kgs. As both drivers were in drunken condition, they were asked to come next day for further enquiry. Material was confiscated and kept at security gate. 
On 19th Oct’23, in the enquiry it was revealed by Mr. Jayaprakash M that, at the time of vehicle loading at Chennai depot, Driver Vijayakumar G kept the few pieces of copper scrap material separately on the roof of driver’s cabin. Driver Vijaykumar kept on rejecting for the allegation and reciprocating allegations on Jayaprakash for hiding the material. Security guard of Jain had found no info on the same.

INCIDENTTITLE: Select at SESA COKE - GUJARAT site at 25 Dec 2023, 10:00 AM

"It was easy for miscreants to scale damaged and low height of the wall.
There is no fencing on wall.
The area has zero illumination.
The area is covered with wild vegetation and zero visibility of cable. "

This was communication to all concerned department .Police complain also logged at Bhachau Police station.
The cable lay out should be grounded / well clamped to avoid easy theft- To be done.                                                         The area needs proper illumination- Done.                                                                The wild vegetation/ tree trimming needs to be cut- to be done.               Wall repairing & fencing to be done.

INCIDENTTITLE: Fraud/Malpractice at VGCB site at 2023-12-16 05:45
INCIDENTDETAILS:
On 15 December 2023 (C Shift) Internal movement shifting of Cargo (Coal) was under process at MVPL (Maritime venture private limited). There were a total of 10 Vehicles performing internal movement from TT-5 Gate to NEW SEW plot. The Vehicle used to enter at TT-5 Gate, Load at stackyard – 4 & Unload after entering NEW SEW plot. Three Vehicles (under mention) did not unload properly at NEW SEW plot & has planned to shift the stolen Cargo at ORCA Stackyard. G. Chinna Babu (ESG) was deployed at NEW SEW plot. 
Root Cause: 
a) No illumination present in the area at night.
b) The complete area is not under any Camera. Coverage. There is no Camera to Monitor NEW SEW plot."
Corrective Action -
a) Illumination has been increased.
STATUS-Implemented
b)Certain security processes at TT5 /NSEW have been redefined.
STATUS-Implemented
c)Proper unloading of cargo is being ensured at all the plots while Internal Shifting.

INCIDENTTITLE: Misdemeanour by Ex-SS at Piparia
INCIDENTDETAILS:
On 26th May 2023, at around 07.40pm SCR operator Abhishek Singh informed SIC Parvathi that logistics person in Piparia Anand had called him for vehicle entry as he could not find the security supervisor. On inquiring it was found that ex-SS Satyaprakash had left his post vacant and left the plant premises, without informing Piparia plant head and/or SIC in Chinchpada.

The Peregrine area officer Ajit and site-incharge of Peregrine Jitendra were informed, and immediately they reached the site and vehicle movement was ensured with due check. At around 08.40pm Supevisor Satyaprakash returned back to premises. It was noticed that the supervisor had consumed alcohol. Peregrine team enquired about the same and asked him to leave the location. 

SIC from Chinchpada reached Piparia at 08.55pm to inquire about the situation and take control of the logistics movement. It was found that Satyaprakash had asked housekeeping person Dilip Kherwa and signed his outgate pass to go outside, on his motorbike. Satyaprakash had ‘wanted to go to Bhilad to find a relative who had run away from home’- as per his statement. Following this, security guard Jayshree took up the post at main gate, but due to inability to perform system work, they were unable to make vehicle entries, because of which logistics SIC Anand had to call Abhishek Singh in Chinchpada.

Night shift SIC Vinush Kadali reported at plant at 09.15pm and took over security operations and all clear was given at 09.20pm.

INCIDENTTITLE: Theft attempt of Copper Scrap in parking yard at silvassa site at 18 Oct 2023, 9:45 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
On 18th October 2023, at around 21:45 hrs., it was come to our notice that two Jain recycling driver were fighting because of their personal intention. Anshu Singh who was deployed as traffic controller immediately called to Logistics shift in charge, Mr. Karuppasamy arrived in the spot immediately and enquired about the conflict between them.as consequence of conflict, one of the drivers (Jay Prakash M) informed to Karuppasamy that other person (Vijayakumar G) have hidden some theft copper material in the truck. Karuppasamy told to security shift in charge Abhishek Singh and Traffic Controller Anshu Singh about this. immediately the team started the vehicle checking and finally they found the hidden copper material in the top roof of the truck. Around 18 kg. of copper scrap material recovered. 
The material was brought to security cabin and follow up investigation was done. All the persons submitted their statements to SIC.

INCIDENTTITLE: Scuffle between 3 persons in ACP washroom at silvassa site at 13 Sep 2023, 11:11 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS: On 11/09/2023 at 11:15 am Mr. Jay Prakash from fabrication mechanical went to the ACP rest room to refresh himself, where PMB helper Mr. Ajit Wagh was washing his face in the wash basin. During that time water splashed on Jay Prakash who was standing near the wash basin. A heated argument ensued between the two and Ajit Wagh groped Jayprakash’s neck tightly, with his hand. At that moment, Mr.Jaglal, fabrication mechanical, co-worker of Jayprakash, walked into the washroom and found them scuffling. Jaglal interfered into the fight, and an argument started between the Ajit Wagh and Jaglal. Then Ajit Wagh caught hold of Jaglal’s neck tightly. Jaglal, feeling suffocated, bit Ajit on his right arm very hard. Following the fight, Ajit Wagh told Jaglal and Jayprakash that this should be reported to the supervisor, but both Jaglal and Jayprakash refused to do so. Ajit Wagh complained to the Monomark supervisor about the events, and the communication reached the Security Shift Officer via the fabrication security guard. The investigation was concluded, with all three persons accepting their misdoings.

INCIDENTTITLE: Incidents against personnel at silvassa site at 16 Sep 2023, 7:00 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS: At 19.05 hours the security team was notified regarding a scuffle that ensued between two Monomark employees working in the Refinery, Mr. Raju Devidas Jadhao and Mr. Ajay Prasad. Both were involved in a verbal duel, followed by physically holding each other’s necks, which was resolved by a VL employee. After investigation at gate, it was found that Raju had asked Ajay to wear apron while shifting plates, due to which Ajay used curse words at him, which led to a verbal fight, and eventually held each other’s neck. We were also notified that Mr. Raju was consuming tobacco during the incident, which was confirmed during investigation, when we found the packet of tobacco in his possession. Statements from both the people were taken, and both have admitted to their mistakes.

INCIDENTTITLE: Theft attempt of blister lug piece at silvassa site at 28 Jun 2023, 7:46 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS: On 28th June 2023, SIC Nandkishore Patel received an intel regarding suspicious activity about Mr. Ramkawal yadav which may happen near CCPC ground floor area. Knowing this, he deployed an ambush SG Shafik Khan at Finance office for vigilance. At 19:46hrs, it was observed that Mr. Ramkawal Yadav, forklift operator, parked the fork lift near lug cutting area at CCPC and sneaked into the lug cutting area after turning off his forklift. He placed a piece of cloth on a copper lug piece and picked the piece up. Further, he hidden the lug piece at the fork of his forklift, he took the forklift and parked it near the CCPC washroom area. He stopped his forklift, took the hidden material out from the fork, hid the piece inside his safety helmet, and he was about to sneak into the washroom. Right in front of the washroom, at 19:52hrs, he was caught by Mr. Nandkishore Patel and SG Shafik Khan, and the material was recovered from his possession. He was interrogated in the Security cabin head Security Ananda and SIC Nandkishore, to which he confessed and provided his statement to the SIC where he accepted his mistake.

INCIDENTTITLE: Theft attempt of electrical equipment at silvassa site at 20 Jun 2023, 6:08 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS: On 20th June 2023, at 18.08hrs, its was understood that electrical work was assigned to Mr. Raunak and Mr.  Divyesh- electrical workmen from Monomark- at car parking yard. As per Mr. Raunak, electrical material (02 LED lights 100W, 03 small tubelights-5W and one 1.5mm copper cable of 50m approx.) was taken from the mini store at DG area and taken out of the plant for the said work, after declaring at the main gate for light fixing in dismantled parking yard. While coming in the plant SG Safik asked about the same, for which Mr. Raunak could not reply satisfactorily. SG Safik informed shift IC Nandkishore Patel about the same. Sensing some foul play, Nandkishore  immediately set out to track the whereabouts of the material. After investigation and questioning the custodians of the material, Nandkishore suspected the material to be in locker room. Both the person’s lockers were checked, and the material was retrieved from Mr. Raunak’s locker. On interrogation, Raunak accepted his misdoing. Same he admitted in his statement. The material was brought to security cabin and follow up investigation was done. Both the persons submitted their statements to SIC.

INCIDENTTITLE: Trespassing through perimeter wall in CCR at silvassa site at 07 Jan 2024, 10:06 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS: On 07th January at 22:06:16 hrs. behind CCR plant location, cast bar area, a person trespassed through the boundary wall no:14 suspected of the theft attempt, while some more persons stood outside the boundary wall keeping watch on the internal person’s movement. The intruder hid himself behind the bushes at the backside of the cast bar area, waiting for the right time to attempt pilferage. This was noticed by the cast bar security post guard Kailash Patil Nathu, he raised an alarm and called QRT, the intruder climbed over the boundary wall and ran away. QRT and shift IC Nandkishore Patel reached the incident spot immediately, they observed some more persons at a distance outside boundary wall. Thereafter, miscreants started pelting stones over the cast bar area. Following this, in consultation with Head Security, immediately the information was given to the police emergency number 100 at 22:22 hrs. Sayli police arrived at the spot at 22:40:05 and visited the scene of the incident with security SIC Nandkishore from outside the area, till the time miscreants disappeared from the site.

INCIDENTDETAILS: On 18th December at 22:44hrs in CCR plant location one of the village persons trespassed through the boundary wall no:14 intentionally for theft attempt, while two more stood outside the boundary wall keeping watch. He was hiding behind the bushes at the backside of the cast bar area, waiting for the right time to attempt pilferage. This was noticed by the cast bar security post guard Gulshed Khan, who when tried to raise alarm and call people, the thief climbed over the boundary wall and ran away. Thereafter, they started pelting stones over the solar panels. Following this, the information was given to the police emergency number 100 at 23:00 hrs and the Naroli police arrived at the spot at 23:20 and investigated the incident which had happened.

INCIDENTTITLE: Scuffle between some drivers of Rathod Transport & Raman Roadways In New parking yard at silvassa site at 08 Feb 2024, 9:45 PM

A brawl had ensued, which led to four people being injured. Shift SIC Abhishek Singh from Peregrine, on being notified, rushed to the scene along with QRT team and controlled the people engaged in to fight and separated them. All clear was called at 00:20hrs.

INCIDENTDETAILS: At around 1400 hrs, during perimeter checking SG Leela Dhar Gogoi detected  a hole in the boundary wall behind the cooling tower 8-9. INCIDENTTITLE: PR - Villagers/ Neighborhood at RAM Agucha site at 19 Mar 2024, 7:30 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
Dated 19th March 2024 at around 0730 hours approximately 25-30 women along with 08-10 men gathered near Shiva Temple located at Rampura Village. After 10-15 minutes of discussion amongst them all women came to the main road and blocked the movement of Men and Material . QRT was stationed at that place , when QRT commander reasoned for the blockade, they told them due to the unemployment in their Rampura Village they have blocked the road.

 

Meanwhile Security Team directed the people to use alternative route from Kothiya to reach the workplace.  The matter was informed to SDM , DSP , CI and Agucha OP In charge for necessary action. Tehsildar & SHO along with Police Force arrived at site by 0830 hrs , CSO RAM and CSR Head RAM also arrived at site by 0845 hrs . Locals cleared the road once CSO and Head CSR arrived at site and stared discussion with them .  After discussion with Administration, HZL Representative & Locals from Rampura Village the matter was resolved amicably.

INCIDENTTITLE: ASSET/PROPERTY at BALCO site at 20 Aug 2023, 1:00 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS: On August 20, 2023, at around 0100 hours, Civ SG Dambar Pandey was assigned to patrol the 400kV Old tower line with the GIS team. While conducting the patrol near tower numbers 50 and 51, he observed the presence of 3 to 4 individuals engaging in suspicious activity near the tower. Without delay, he relayed this information to the CSOC (Central Security Operations Centre) and requested backup since only three members were on patrol at the time. Upon further observation, it was discovered that there were 7 to 8 individuals involved in theft. The CSOC promptly transmitted a message to notify NDO Mr. Manish Sarathe and Plant-2 shift I/C Mr. Raghav Tomar. They arrived at the scene and, upon detecting the security team's presence, the miscreants hastily departed the location. Seizing the opportunity provided by the darkness, they fled from the location, leaving the stolen materials in their haste.

INCIDENTDETAILS: On June 6th, 2023, at around 0845 hrs., Security Personnel Mr. MD Tiwari,  deployed at Alumina Gate, reported an incident of proxy punching involving Mr. Jayanarayan, UID No. 1594375, worker of M/S KEC International Ltd. Allegedly, Mr. Jayanarayan was found in possession of two gate passes: his own and one that belonged to Pawan Singh (UID-1595826), enabling him to perform Proxy Punch. The security team apprehended Mr. Jayanarayan at Alumina Gate with the two gate passes. Subsequent investigation revealed that Mr. Pawan Singh had been on leave for the past three days and had not entered the plant during that time. Mr. Pawan Singh had entrusted his gate pass to Mr. Jayanarayan  for punching at the gate. While Mr. Jayanarayan possessed his own gate pass, he also punched Mr. Pawan Singh's gate pass at Alumina Gate. As a result, both supervisors, Mr. Chandrapal Singh (Pawan Singh's supervisor) and Mr. Karthick Das (Jayanarayan supervisor), were also summoned for the investigation.

INCIDENTTITLE: Material at BALCO site at 13 Jun 2023, 7:45 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS: During a routine inspection of vehicles at the Alumina Four Wheeler Gate on June 13th, 2023, Security Guard Mr. Rajeev Ranjan reported an incident. At approximately 19:45 hours, he discovered Fiber optic patch cords in a project vehicle with the registration number CG12 AQ 8379. The vehicle was being driven by Mr. Satyendra Sahu, who is an employee of ABB Power Products and Systems and works under Hitachi Energy India Limited. Upon receiving the aforementioned incident report, a subsequent investigation was conducted, uncovering that the fiber optic patch cords were being utilized for panel relay communication work. On June 13th, 2023, these patch cords were initially stored in Package One Container near the GIS area. As the work progressed, it was planned to relocate the fiber patch cords from Package One Container to Package Two Container near the Petrol Pump, using the vehicle with the registration number CG12 AQ 8379. Both of these containers, Package One and Package Two, belonged to Hitachi Energy. Consequently, the responsibility for loading the fiber optic patch cords into the vehicle with the number CG12 AQ 8379 was assigned to a worker named Mr. Jahed Alam. Mr. Jahed Alam successfully loaded the fiber optic patch cord material onto the vehicle CG12 AQ 8379. However, upon reaching the vehicle's destination, Mr. Jahed Alam failed to unload the fiber optic patch cord material from the vehicle. As a result, the fiber optic patch cord material remained inside the vehicle CG12 AQ 8379 until its arrival at the Alumina Gate. Furthermore, during the departure, the driver, Mr. Satyendra Sahu, also neglected to inspect the vehicle, which led to his apprehension by the Alumina Gate Security. The information regarding the incident was confirmed with the relevant department. Statements were obtained from all individuals involved, and a communication regarding ownership of fibre optic patch cord Material was obtained from reliability project department.

INCIDENTTITLE: Material at BALCO site at 03 Sep 2023, 2:30 AM

These individuals were observed carrying materials while exiting the plant perimeter wall foxhole. CSOC promptly conveyed this information to the Plant-1 InCharge, leading to the immediate redirection of the Quick Response Team (QRT) to the location, both from outside and inside the plant. Simultaneously, the V Patrolling team (Traffic) was alerted to respond promptly from the outside. Upon receiving the information, our security teams, deployed from both inside and outside, cordoned off the area and initiated a thorough search operation. However, the perpetrators managed to flee the location before our teams could apprehend them. Further investigation revealed that the materials-Concrete Trimme Pipe (3 M long) - 2,Trimme Wrench & Head,MS Pipe - 21 nos,Prefabricated Security Booths - 3 were missing from the project area, as reported by the L&T supervisor during the morning inspection.

INCIDENTTITLE: PR - Villagers/ Neighborhood at IRON ORE KARNATAKA site at 2024-03-23 13:36
INCIDENTDETAILS:
On dated 23rd March 2024, at around 10:15Hrs, Johan mines canter vehicle movement started via Gate 3, Common gate and Kadleguddu route with Police force deployment in Kadleguddu village. At around 11:30Hrs, 05 loaded canter vehicles were released from mines and hold by village peoples at  outskirts of Kadleguddu village. Police intervene the matter however they not agree to released the canter vehicle. At around 13:15Hrs, Police team return back the loaded vehicles from Kadleguddu village. Since canter vehicles were not released from Kadleguddu village, at around 1335 hours, canter drivers  and local village peoples from Meghalahalli village blocked the admin building main gate and gate 3 with the help of canter vehicles and bikes. Because of the John Mines canter vehicle-Kadlaguddu village dispute, they demanded  that no Kadleguddu employee be permitted to enter the mine area to carry out their job. To avoid forced entry, the security team promptly closed the admin block main entrance and Gate #3 main gate. Latter informed to Senior management, Bharamsagara  Police station and  Extra security guards deployed at location. About 1400 hours, seregire police arrived at location and discussion started.
After the discussion, about 1540 hours, vehicle movement resumed at admin main gate & Gate # 3 main gate. Nonetheless, the canter drivers and people of Meghalahalli were seated close to the main gate of the administrative office. Around 1800 hours, CI Bhramasagara arrived at the admin block. Following a meeting between senior management and the villagers, union members, and police, the situation was settled, and at approximately 1840 hours, villagers exited the administration block.

The security team swiftly responded, arriving at the scene within 4 minutes, conducting a thorough search, and apprehending two suspects identified as Sunil Kherwar (14 years) and Samir Dewangan (18 years). Subsequently, they were handed over to the BALCO Police station with a written complaint.

INCIDENTTITLE: Material at BALCO site at 07 Oct 2023, 10:30 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS: At approximately 2230 hours on October 7th, 2023, Intelligence team received information from an informant that a worker, identified as Kuldeep Saw, employed at the plant, and recognized by his distinctive orange uniform, was involved in the unauthorized removal and subsequent sale of safety Personal Protective Equipment (PPEs) from the premises. The informant further revealed that Kuldeep Saw had established a pattern of carrying out this illicit activity and was planning to exit the plant between 0100 and 0200 hours on the same night. In response to this information, we immediately initiated a response protocol by deploying a Quick Response Team (QRT) along with the Plant-1 shift in-charge to apprehend the suspect and prevent any further unauthorized removal of safety PPEs. At approximately 0125 hours, the deployed QRT and the Plant-1 in-charge successfully apprehended Mr. Kuldeep Saw, who was in possession of the stolen safety PPEs. Mr. Saw's UID is 1551040, and he is employed by M/s Liz Aluminium Services Private Ltd. During questioning, Mr. Saw admitted to engaging in this activity, confirming that he had been involved in such incidents in 3-4 times in the past, having taken out safety PPEs from the plant and selling them in the external market.

INCIDENTTITLE: Material at BALCO site at 25 Sep 2023, 6:01 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS: On 25.09.2023, at approximately 1801 hours, our intelligence team received critical information regarding the vehicle registered under CG12BJ2780, which was allegedly involved in a plot to steal copper plate, copper angle, and copper rod from the rodding area. This vital intelligence was promptly relayed to Mr. Arbind Mehta, the shift in charge for Plant-1. Around 1830 hours, Mr. Mehta successfully located the vehicle at the alumina gate. Subsequent inspection of the vehicle revealed the presence of copper plate, copper angle, and copper rod concealed beneath the backseat and underneath the vehicle's carpet. Upon conducting a thorough investigation, it was determined that Mr. Brjesh Pratap Singh (Employee ID: 1598119, L&T), who holds the position of supervisor at L&T's rodding site, had observed his colleague, Vishekhar, in the act of removing copper materials from the site. Seizing this illicit opportunity, Mr. Singh decided to pilfer some copper materials from the site and enlisted the assistance of his coworker, Anil Yadav (UID: 1599165, L&T). Together, they transported the stolen copper materials in vehicle (CG12BJ2780), only to be apprehended at the alumina gate, caught red-handed with the purloined goods.

INCIDENTTITLE: SOP- LSR Violation at VGCB site at 01 Aug 2023, 5:30 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
At around 0530h ,the Vaaman workers were told that vehicles with proper documents will only be allowed to go inside .The workers got agitated and started shouting on security personnel .Later the workers refused to go for work quoting lack of basic amenities in plant i.e., no changing room, less no of washrooms, canteen. 
Root Cause: Workers had few demands which were pending since long time and their non fulfillment gave them a chance to create issue.
CAPA: 
Corrective Action: 
1. CSO ,SO and vaamman HR reached the spot and convinced workers to go for work. 
2.  Workers given a time period of 1 month to arrange their two wheeler documents .
3. Management included the project of constructing a new changing room and a canteen for Vaaman workers. 
STATUS-Under progress
Preventive Action:
1. Security personal trained to handle such situations in a poilite manner
2. R& R program conducted for vaaman workers.
3. Regular lectures/Briefings conducted on morale and motivation.

INCIDENTTITLE: Unsafe Act at VAL J site at 24 Jan 2024, 8:40 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS: At around 2040 hrs, an operator named Mr. Chetananda Behera working in IPP Truck Tippler area reported that a Coal Truck Driver  (Vehicle No. OD 16M 0262)  named Mr. Vikash Kumar, working under the Contractor M/S Matarani Transport attempted to commit suicide in the IPP Truck Tippler area after unloading his truck at the truck tippler path no. 4. It was informed that he went below the vehicle and jumped inside the coal hopper of path no. 3. The operator deputed at the site observed this incident and immediately stopped the operations. The said operator along with two other operators working in that area took the aforesaid coal truck driver out from the coal hopper and rescued safely. He was taken to the medical center. IP was tested for the alcohol, drug and the test reports were found negative. Immediately the police was called. IP was brought to the police and complaint was given. Police checked the CCTV footage. IP was handed over to the vehicle owner by the police safely. The vehicle was safely parked in the TPP area for further investigation.

INCIDENTTITLE: Traffic at VAL J site at 18 May 2023, 2:30 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS: Today at 0230 hours, a coal-loaded truck bearing registration number OD16K2551 of Maa Kalika transport collided with three empty coal trucks that were moving (in a queue for weighment) towards the Guest House Weighbridge. The impact resulted in damage to the driver cabin and front glass of all vehicles involved. No injuries have been reported from this incident.Alcohol tests were conducted for all drivers, and the results were negative, the drug test of two drivers was found positive (parked vehicles). It is essential to note that the negligent behaviour of the driver of the coal-loaded truck, who was found sleeping while operating the vehicle, led to this unfortunate incident. Three empty coal vehicles have been removed from the spot for smooth traffic flow. As per road safety consequence management Challan was issued against the driver Pradip Kumar of OD16F4068 and Rahul Kumar & Gajendra Behera (Illicit Drugs)Mail communicated to user department and vehicles are on hold.

INCIDENTTITLE: Select at ENABLING site at 04 Dec 2023, 12:03 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS: Around 12:03 AM, QRT 03 notified the shift IC regarding the vandalism of copper electric cables along the railway project near Bokaro Farm House on Vedanta Lane, presumably for theft. Upon receiving the alert, both QRT and the Shift IC promptly arrived at the location. A comprehensive search of the area revealed that the electric cable had been severed at two points, but the culprits were not found on-site. The QRT team, alongside the Shift IC, retrieved the damaged cable. No further incidents of cable theft have been reported thus far. The Railway Project Department has been informed of the situation. The Shift IC swiftly assigned a guard to the area and instructed the QRT to stay vigilant. Based on the investigation conducted by both the Shift IC and QRT, there is a suspicion that the local guard deployed at the site might be implicated in this incident.

INCIDENTTITLE: ASSET/PROPERTY at VAB site at 01 Feb 2024, 11:00 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS: On 01st Feb 2024 at around 11:00 hrs. Mr. Sameer Teli & Mr. Paresh Ghadi of barge operations team boarded the barge named Charao for their routine check at Amona jetty when the barge was being loaded with pig iron. They checked and found that the diesel main tank reading was 1950 lts, service tank port side reading was 818 lts and starboard side tank was 815 lts. However, the diesel tank readings given by the driver in the morning were 1500 lts for the main tank, 800 lts for the portside service tank and 800 lts for starboard side tank. This amounts to a difference of 450 lts. It is very clear that the readings were deliberately wrongly reported by the barge driver. A difference of max 50 Ltrs is acceptable due to barge loading operations going on but not a difference of 450 lts.

INCIDENTTITLE: Fire at VAB site at 27 Mar 2024, 12:30 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS: Fire broke out due to a short circuit in the 11kV line located behind the C Gate container area. The fire rapidly spread due to dry grass in the vicinity. Fire brigade was notified and arrived at 13:03 hours. Before their arrival, security personnel played a crucial role in firefighting, clearing parked vehicles from the vicinity of the fire. Additionally, a water tanker was called, and individuals joined in spraying water to extinguish the flames. To ensure efficient operations, the movement of raw material through the gate was temporarily halted to prevent unnecessary traffic.

INCIDENTTITLE: Select at IRON AND POWER site at 22 Aug 2023, 3:00 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
At around 15:00 hrs, Mr. Palash Kar, (Section Head WRM & Roll Shop) informed Security team that TC rings Scrap had been stolen from their store worth approx Rs 35,00,000. After which Security team reached on the spot & asked Mr. Suresh Kumar (Store Incharge WRM ) about the case. He said that He had checked the material on last Saturday during his 'G' Shift, after which they saw it today when the lock was missing from the Iron box & TC rings were missing.  "During Enquiry & temporary investigation Security team observed the area & the following observations were found:-  1.) Key of the Scrap store was missing since last one week. They were temporarily locking the Aluminum door by using an alternate option without any key. Today the store was found open.  
2.) Window glass of the store was found broken, which can be the crime scene as 1 TC ring was found outside the same glass window.  
3.) Store is not under CCTV coverage.
4.) Window was only covered with glass. window was not covered with grill due to which the TC rings were easily taken out. Weight of 1 scrap TC ring was 2Kg. Shift IC suggested, Mr. Anand Reddy (HOD) to lodge an FIR, Also to provide names of suspected person for investigation & for further record.                                                      "

INCIDENTTITLE: PR-Villagers/Neighbourhood at VAL J site at 16 Oct 2023, 5:05 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS: At around 0505 hrs,10-15 villagers of Burkhamunda Village gathered at the Bhurkamunda Gate. They stopped the employees' movement (A Shift) in the pretext of Vedanta's unfulfilled promise to resettle and develop Burkhamunda village. This was discussed with the company six months ago, but Vedanta has not responded to or taken action so far. With the intervention of the CSR team, it was resolved at about 0947 hrs. normalized movement.
